message:
fix(glamour): use ANSI-aware width calculation in word wrapping

Fixes word wrapping miscalculation when text contains ANSI escape
sequences (colors, styles). Previously used unicode_width which counted
escape sequences as visible characters, causing premature line breaks.

Changes:
1. Adds visible_width() function that parses and skips:
   - CSI sequences (\x1b[...m for colors/styles)
   - OSC sequences (\x1b]...BEL for terminal titles etc.)
   - Simple escape sequences (\x1b7, \x1b>)

2. Updates word_wrap() to use visible_width() instead of UnicodeWidthStr

3. Reverts ASCII style inline code backticks - the rendering test shows
   that ASCII mode intentionally renders code as plain text, not with
   visible delimiters (this differs from the previous commit assumption)

4. Updates test expectation to match actual ASCII rendering behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
